pages.text: $(bold)Mechanical Bearing$() — feed it rotation and it spins whatever is glued in front of it. This is how a $(bold)Windmill$() works: glue sails to the bearing, and wind (well, motion) spins it into a power source.$(br2)Point it the other way — drive the bearing and it turns the structure for you, for rotating displays, lighthouses, or moving platforms.
pages.title: Mechanical Bearing

pages.text: $(li)$(bold)Windmill Bearing$() — the variant tuned for sail assemblies; more sail blocks means more stress capacity.$(li)$(bold)Clockwork Bearing$() — drives clock hands from the time of day, for working clocks.$(br2)A bearing only assembles its contraption when given a redstone pulse or power, depending on type.
pages.title: Bearing Types
